meta.name: TAS-20 Toronto Alexithymia Scale
meta.description: The TAS-20 is a 20-item self-report scale measuring three dimensions of alexithymia: difficulty identifying feelings, difficulty describing feelings, and externally-oriented thinking.
meta.author: Bagby RM, Parker JDA, Taylor GJ (1994)
meta.reference: Bagby RM, Parker JDA, Taylor GJ. The twenty-item Toronto Alexithymia Scale: I. Item selection and cross-validation of the factor structure. J Psychosom Res. 1994;38(1):23-32.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much you agree or disagree with each of the following statements based on how you generally feel.

questions.text: I am often confused about what emotion I am feeling.
It is difficult for me to find the right words for my feelings.
I have physical sensations that even doctors don't understand.
I am able to describe my feelings easily.
I prefer to analyze problems rather than just describe them.
When I am upset, I don't know if I am sad, frightened, or angry.
I am often puzzled by sensations in my body.
I prefer to just let things happen rather than to understand why they turned out that way.
I have feelings that I can't quite identify.
Being in touch with emotions is essential.
I find it hard to describe how I feel about people.
People tell me to describe my feelings more.
I don't know what's going on inside me.
I often don't know why I am angry.
I prefer talking to people about their daily activities rather than their feelings.
I prefer watching light entertainment shows rather than psychological dramas.
It is difficult for me to reveal my innermost feelings, even to close friends.
I can feel close to someone, even in moments of silence.
I find examination of my feelings useful in solving personal problems.
Looking for hidden meanings in movies or plays distracts from their enjoyment.

ranges.label: No Alexithymia
ranges.description: Your score indicates good emotional awareness and the ability to identify, describe, and reflect on your feelings. You appear comfortable with emotional experiences and can effectively communicate your inner states to others.
suggestions: Continue developing your emotional intelligence through self-reflection and mindfulness
Maintain open emotional communication in your relationships
Use your emotional awareness skills to support others who may struggle with identifying feelings

ranges.label: Possible Alexithymia
ranges.description: Your score falls in the borderline range, suggesting some difficulty with emotional awareness. You may sometimes struggle to distinguish between emotions and physical sensations, or find it hard to describe your feelings to others. This pattern is worth monitoring.
suggestions: Practice emotion labeling — try to name your feelings several times throughout the day
Keep an emotion journal to track patterns in how you experience and express feelings
Explore body-based awareness practices like yoga or progressive muscle relaxation to connect physical sensations with emotions
Consider psychoeducation about emotions through books or workshops

ranges.label: Alexithymia
ranges.description: Your score suggests significant difficulty identifying, describing, and processing emotions. Alexithymia can affect relationships, physical health, and psychological well-being. It is associated with higher rates of depression, anxiety, and somatic complaints, and may benefit from professional support.
suggestions: Consider seeking professional help — therapies such as mentalization-based therapy or emotion-focused therapy can help develop emotional awareness
Practice structured emotion identification exercises using emotion wheels or feeling charts
Explore body-awareness practices to develop connections between physical sensations and emotional states
Engage in creative expression (art, music, writing) as alternative channels for emotional processing
Build a support system of trusted people who can help you recognize and process emotional experiences

difficulty_identifying.label: Low
difficulty_identifying.description: You show good ability to identify your emotions and distinguish them from physical sensations. This skill supports effective emotional regulation and helps you respond appropriately to your emotional needs.

difficulty_identifying.label: Moderate
difficulty_identifying.description: You show some difficulty recognizing and identifying your emotions. You may sometimes confuse emotional arousal with physical symptoms, or feel unsure about what emotion you are experiencing.

difficulty_identifying.label: High
difficulty_identifying.description: You have significant difficulty identifying your emotions. This can lead to confusion about internal states, difficulty knowing what you need, and may manifest as unexplained physical complaints. Emotion identification training may be beneficial.

difficulty_describing.label: Low
difficulty_describing.description: You can effectively describe your feelings to others. This skill supports clear emotional communication, helps others understand your needs, and facilitates emotional processing through verbalization.

difficulty_describing.label: Moderate
difficulty_describing.description: You show some difficulty putting your emotions into words. This may lead to miscommunication in relationships or a sense that others don’t fully understand your experiences. Expanding your emotional vocabulary can help.

difficulty_describing.label: High
difficulty_describing.description: You find it quite difficult to describe your emotional experiences to others. This can create barriers in relationships and limit the benefits of social support. Working with a therapist on emotional expression skills is recommended.

externally_oriented.label: Low
externally_oriented.description: You show good orientation toward inner emotional life rather than exclusively focusing on external events. This introspective capacity supports self-reflection, emotional depth, and psychological insight.

externally_oriented.label: Moderate
externally_oriented.description: You show a moderate tendency to focus on external events rather than inner emotional experiences. While practical thinking has its merits, balancing it with emotional reflection can enhance self-understanding.

externally_oriented.label: High
externally_oriented.description: You strongly prefer focusing on external events and practical details rather than internal emotional experiences. This externally oriented thinking style can limit emotional self-awareness and make psychological processing more difficult.
